sections.title: Definition of the product
sections.text: Edible cassava ( Manihot esculenta Crantz) flour is the product prepared from dried cassava chips or paste by a pounding, grinding or milling process, followed by sifting to separate the fibre from the flour. In the case of edible cassava flour prepared from bitter cassava ( Manihot utilissima Pohl), detoxification is carried out by soaking the tubers in water for a few days, before they undergo drying in the form of whole, pounded tuber (paste) or in small pieces.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION > Definition of the product
number=2.1
level=2
Edible cassava ( Manihot esculenta Crantz) flour is the product prepared from dried cassava chips or paste by a pounding, grinding or milling process, followed by sifting to separate the fibre from the flour. In the case of edible cassava flour prepared from bitter cassava ( Manihot utilissima Pohl), detoxification is carried out by soaking the tubers in water for a few days, before they undergo drying in the form of whole, pounded tuber (paste) or in small pieces.

page_spans.text: Edible cassava ( Manihot esculenta Crantz) flour is the product prepared from dried cassava chips or paste by a pounding, grinding or milling process, followed by sifting to separate the fibre from the flour. In the case of edible cassava flour prepared from bitter cassava ( Manihot utilissima Pohl), detoxification is carried out by soaking the tubers in water for a few days, before they undergo drying in the form of whole, pounded tuber (paste) or in small pieces.

sections.title: Quality factors – general
sections.text:
3.1.1 Edible cassava flour shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
3.1.2 Edible cassava flour shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
3.1.3 Edible cassava flour shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: Information for non -retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sections.text: In those instances where more than one factor limit and/or method of analysis is given, we strongly recommend that users specify the appropriate limit and method of analysis. sentences.original: Edible cassava ( Manihot esculenta Crantz) flour is the product prepared from dried cassava chips or paste by a pounding, grinding or milling process, followed by sifting to separate the fibre from the flour.

prefilter.reason: Describes process relevant to cassava flour.

classification.sentence: In the case of edible cassava flour prepared from bitter cassava ( Manihot utilissima Pohl), detoxification is carried out by soaking the tubers in water for a few days, before they undergo drying in the form of whole, pounded tuber (paste) or in small pieces.
classification.pattern: Conditional clause followed by an obligation description

components.condition: edible cassava flour prepared from bitter cassava (Manihot utilissima Pohl)
components.obligation: detoxification by soaking tubers in water for days before drying

classification.justification: Contains a conditional clause setting a specific treatment obligation for certain raw material; no matching template found, structure involves condition plus mandatory action.
notes: Typical processing step with conditional obligation

pages.summary: Página inicial do documento com título principal e cabeçalho, sem seções estruturadas visíveis.
Página sem seções estruturais claramente identificáveis. Contém cabeçalho e texto introdutório sem títulos ou subtítulos.

A página apresenta seis seções principais numeradas (1 a 6) com diversos subtítulos numerados de segundo e terceiro níveis. O texto está associado corretamente aos títulos e subtítulos correspondentes. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas para essas seções.

A página apresenta a seção 7 LABELLING com dois subtítulos 7.1 e 7.2, seguidos da seção 8 METHODS OF SAMPLING. A seção 7 continua na próxima página, conforme indicado pela ausência de fechamento claro. O texto está corretamente associado a cada título e subtítulo com hierarquia clara para uso em RAG.

A página apresenta duas seções principais: ANNEX e NOTES, ambas em nível 1. ANNEX continua conteúdo da seção anterior (marca is_continuation_from_previous_page=true) e NOTES é uma nova seção. Texto associado está limpo e sem cabeçalho repetitivo. Não há níveis hierárquicos inferiores visíveis nesta página.
